mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where industrial production increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation reading above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 50% of high-inflation trigger months across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Rank IC over consecutive 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of positive GDP growth over a 4-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.8% signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when industrial production drops below the 0.8% threshold.
